Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

    the verification path, slate as separate pace if structural or out-of-scope,
    capture as itch if speculative.

    initial debug per operator).

    routing.

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

**Recent neighborhood (parallel coding/testing track):**

- BBAAO (buf-fact-multi-foundation): landed (commit bffc58cb)
- BBAAN (cult-verb refactor): paddock-settled per rev-3 "Settled decisions"; code pending

### What BBAAQ actually is

A **spec-only sweep** — no code, no bash, no Rust. Four doc surfaces:

1. **BUS0** — multi-fact convention (BBAAO landed): new quoin family for type-as-extension, closed registry, dual-write semantics; `BUF_EXT_*` convention; BUK/RB boundary
2. **RBS0** — cult-verb concepts (BBAAN paddock-settled, code pending): invest/divest/muster triad, `rbw-a[rd][IDm]` colophon scheme, RBYC/RBZ identities, behavioral contracts

**Out of scope this pace:** code changes, handbook tracks rbho/rbhp (sibling pace ₢BBAAR), single-fact spec.

### Notable wrinkle

Surface 2 (RBS0 cult-verb) authors from BBAAN's *paddock-settled vocabulary* without waiting for code to land. Risk noted in docket: a late paddock-revisit at BBAAN mount could force a small follow-on edit here. Accepted in exchange for parallelizing with the testing thread.

### Silks assessment

`multi-fact-cult-verb-spec` is accurate but lossy — drops the depot-identity banner (surface 4) added in rev-2. Survives as adequate; rename optional. Leaving as-is.

### Proposed approach

- **Sequential, single officium** — each surface needs careful reading of landed code (rbgc_Constants.sh, buf_fact.sh, BBAAN docket rev-3). Coherence across surfaces matters more than parallelism.
- **Order**: surface 4 (mechanical banner sweep, smallest) → surface 1 (BUS0, code landed) → surface 2 (RBS0 cult-verb, paddock-settled) → surface 3 (README glossary, falls out of surface 2 vocabulary).
- **Model tier**: opus — MCM linked-term shape requires concept-model judgment (attribute reference + anchor + voicing), not mechanical edits.
- **Real-time review at each step** per docket constraint. No batching, no pre-engineered choices.

### Ready to start?

If yes: I'll begin with surface 4 (RBS0 + RBSDE depot-identity banner sweep) since it's the most mechanical and lets us warm into the spec-touching cadence before the harder MCM authoring on surfaces 1 and 2. Shall I proceed?

108	.. {rbbc_require}
109	HTTP 200 response confirming IAM propagation complete
110	.. {rbbc_fatal}
111	if timeout: "IAM propagation timeout - artifactregistry API not accessible"
112	
113	//axhos_step
114	. *Create Build Bucket*:
115	.. Construct bucket name: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-bucket`
116	.. {rbbc_call}
117	link:https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/insert[storage.buckets.insert] with:
118	... name: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-bucket`
119	... location: `{rbrr_gcp_region}`
120	... project: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
121	... lifecycle rule: delete objects after 1 day
122	.. {rbbc_store}
123	`«BUILD_BUCKET»` as created bucket name
124	
125	//axhos_step
126	. *Create Container Repository*:
127	.. Construct repository name: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-repository`
128	.. {rbbc_submit}
129	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/create[artifactregistry.repositories.create] with:
130	... repositoryId: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-repository`
131	... format: `DOCKER`
132	... location: `{rbrr_gcp_region}`
133	... parent: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/locations/{rbrr_gcp_region}`
134	.. {rbbc_await}
135	returned operation
136	.. {rbbc_store}
137	`«GAR_REPOSITORY»` as created repository name
138	
139	//axhos_step
140	. *Create
141	{rbtr_mason}
142	Service Account*:
143	.. NOTE: The
144	{rbtr_mason}
145	replaces Cloud Build's implicit service account to provide deterministic permissions and predictable authorization within the depot infrastructure.
146	.. {rbbc_call}
147	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[iam.serviceAccounts.create] with:
148	... accountId: `mason-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»`
149	... displayName: `Mason for RB Depot: «INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»`
150	... project: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
151	.. {rbbc_store}
152	`«MASON_EMAIL»` from response `.email` field
153	
154	//axhos_step
155	. *Configure
156	{rbtr_mason}
157	Permissions*:
158	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_repo} on `«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
159	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.writer`
160	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket} on `«BUILD_BUCKET»`
161	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/storage.objectViewer`
162	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} on `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
163	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/viewer`
164	... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/logging.logWriter`
165	
166	//axhos_step
167	. *Enable Cloud Build service agent to impersonate
168	{rbtr_mason}*:
169	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa} on `«MASON_EMAIL»`
170	.. Add member: `serviceAccount:service-«PROJECT_NUMBER»@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com`
171	.. Grant role: `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator`
172	.. NOTE: Cloud Build service agent is Google-provisioned (auto-created when Cloud Build API is enabled), not a user-created service account. The `tokenCreator` role enables build steps running as {rbtr_mason} (via the build JSON's `serviceAccount` field) to generate fresh access tokens for GCP API calls — required for GAR authentication (docker login, crane push). This is distinct from `serviceAccountUser` (actAs), which the {rbtr_director} holds on {rbtr_mason} for submitting builds.
173	.. {rbbc_require}
174	IAM binding successful
175	
176	//axhoot_typed_output
177	{rbtgi_depot}
178	. *Display Depot Configuration*:
179	.. {rbbc_show}
180	depot creation successful
181	.. {rbbc_show}
182	required RBRR configuration values:
183	... `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
184	... `RBRR_GCP_REGION={rbrr_gcp_region}`
185	... `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY=«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
186	... `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM=«WORKER_POOL_STEM»`
187	.. {rbbc_show}
188	Mason service account: `«MASON_EMAIL»`
189	
190	//axhoot_typed_output
191	{rbtgi_depot}
192	. *Update Depot Tracking*:
193	.. Execute
194	{rbtoe_depot_list_update}
195	to refresh depot project tracking
196	
197	//axhoc_completion
198	Successful execution creates depot infrastructure with configured build bucket, container repository, Mason service account, and private worker pool.
199	Proceed to
200	{rbtgo_governor_mantle}
201	to create the
202	{rbtr_governor}
203	for this depot.

2760	Must be set to the pool base name created by
2761	{rbtgo_depot_levy}
2762	for this depot.
2763	Builds always use a private pool; the default shared pool is not supported.
2764	`options.machineType` is omitted from the build JSON — machine type is a property of the pool resource.
2765	
2766	NOTE: *Private pool is always required.*
2767	Private pools are a prerequisite for VPC Service Controls and `NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS` hardening (see `RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc`).
2768	Pricing is region-dependent: in us-central1/us-east4, private pool e2-standard-2 matches the default pool rate ($0.006/build-minute); in other regions the private pool rate is up to 1.4× higher.
2769	This cost is accepted because the security prerequisite is non-negotiable and maintaining two code paths (conditional pool vs. default) doubles configuration surface.
2770	+

2772	the default shared pool uses Cloud Build enum values (`E2_HIGHCPU_8`, `N1_HIGHCPU_32`) in `options.machineType` of the build JSON;

2774	Valid private pool families: `e2`, `n2d`, `c3` (availability varies by region).
2775	Since Recipe Bottle uses only private pools, only the Compute Engine format is valid for
2776	{rbrr_gcb_machine_type}.

(1) rbtdrc_crucible.rs: removed RBTDRC_ARK_SUFFIX_{IMAGE,VOUCH}; added RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_{IMAGE,VOUCH} and RBTDRC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS matching their RBGC_ counterparts; rewrote the four call sites to emit the new locator grammar. Wrest locators (relative-to-cloud-prefix since rbfr_wrest prepends RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX itself): 'hallmarks/<hallmark>/<basename>:<hallmark>'. Full local refs (prefixed, match what wrest pulled to local cache): '<gar_root>/<prefixed_ark_stem>/<basename>:<hallmark>'. Per-mode symmetry enforced: each mode now reads its own HALLMARK + GAR_ROOT + ARK_STEM facts and builds refs uniformly. (2) rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh: added GAR_ROOT and ARK_STEM fact emissions to rbfd_kludge to match conjure/bind/graft's fact contract — previously kludge only emitted HALLMARK, forcing Rust consumers to reconstruct the stem via string manipulation on conjure's fact. Per-mode fact emission is now uniform; downstream consumers read the same three facts regardless of mode. (3) RBS0-SpecTop.adoc:1884: fixed stale 'Ark stem (vessel:hallmark)' description that Agent A missed during Notch 3 — rewrote to match the actual emission shape '«PREFIX»hallmarks/«HALLMARK»'. (4) RBSAG-ark_graft.adoc:114: aligned ark_stem description with RBSAC's wording — Agent C described the stem without cloud prefix, Agent B described it with; the bash truth (includes prefix via RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT) matches Agent B. Verification: cargo build succeeded (theurge crate, dev profile); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh returns 90/90 green (47 enrollment-validation + 21 regime-validation + 7 regime-smoke + 15 handbook-render) matching Notch 2b baseline with no regression. Crucible suite (which exercises the edited fixture) not run — requires container runtime and live GAR credentials; flagging for user-side or foray-based verification in the close summary of AAK. The hard-cutover contract is now genuinely complete: no old grammar survives in live code paths. Two flagged follow-ups from Notch 3 remain: (a) Agent A's 'probe' verb choice in RBS0 ~1832 (shape-level; RBSCL is the authoritative description so cross-reference reads consistent); (b) the wrest locator contract split — fact_ark_stem includes cloud prefix, wrest's locator input does not — surfaces as a Rust-side conceptual tax (Rust builds two different string forms from the same semantic concept). A future cleanup could unify (e.g., split into RELATIVE_STEM + PREFIX facts or fold cloud prefix into GAR_ROOT). Scope-out for AAK: the contract asymmetry doesn't produce incorrect behavior, just more format! calls. Closes ₢A_AAK's cutover-discipline contract.
93c651f2 jjb:1011-5c71dab2:₢A_AAK:n: Notch 3 of ₢A_AAK — spec sweep completing the GAR categorical-layout migration. 18 spec files updated to match the Notch 2b code cutover. Heaviest change is RBSAA: the abjure algorithm description collapses from a per-suffix HEAD/DELETE dance (241 lines, 5-6 hardcoded suffixes, per-artifact existence + orphan-detection branches + individual DELETE steps) to enumerate-then-delete via GAR REST /packages (122 lines, single list-filter-loop-delete flow that naturally tolerates graft's missing pouch). RBSAV dissolves the vouches aggregator: _RBGV_VOUCHES_PACKAGE attribute row deleted entirely, dual-tag push grammar (VESSEL:HALLMARK-vouch + vouches:HALLMARK-vouch) replaced by single package under hallmarks/HALLMARK/vouch with subtree navigation for hallmark-only lookup. RBSAE reflects the enshrine anchor-as-path-segment shift — the `enshrine:anchor` tag-on-shared-package grammar becomes `enshrines/anchor:anchor` peer packages under a plural namespace, symmetric with hallmarks/ and reliquaries/. RBSCB rebuilds its Cloud Build tag-scheme table to show full <prefix>hallmarks/HALLMARK/<basename>:HALLMARK shape with a new attest row capturing the single-package-with-per-platform-tags pattern. RBSCL and RBSDV classification algorithms key off basename presence under hallmark subtrees instead of tag-suffix matching; health-state semantics (vouched/pending/incomplete) preserved. RBSDI gains `_RBGN_CLOUD_PREFIX` Cloud Build substitution docs plus the new reliquaries/<date>/<tool>:<date> path example. The remaining 12 files receive proportionally lighter edits: basename-shape tag grammar in RBSAC/RBSAS/RBSAG/RBSAK/RBSIJ/RBSCC, subtree-navigation prose in RBSAP/RBSGS replacing the vouches-superdirectory mechanism, attribute renames in RBSAB (_RBGA_ARK_SUFFIX_* → _RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_*) and RBSTB (_RBGY_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE → _RBGY_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE) with their RBGC_* back-references. All 7 binding verification greps return zero: RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_, RBGC_VOUCHES_PACKAGE, :${HALLMARK}-(image|vouch|pouch|about|diags) hyphen-suffix concat, enshrine:anchor old tag grammar, 'vouches (superdirectory|package|aggregator)' aggregator phrasing, «HALLMARK»-(image|vouch|pouch|about|diags) stale substitution grammar, and {vessel}:{hallmark}-X. Agent A used verb 'probe' in RBS0 ~1832 re: tally detection mechanism without inspecting rbfl_tally implementation; RBSCL (Agent D) is the authoritative description and reads consistently. Spec-only change — no bash/Rust modified, no test suite run (Notch 2b already banked 90/90 fast-suite green). Closes Notch 3 of the 3-notch commit sequence in the groomed AAK plan; AAK's spec-side contract is now complete. Commit size 77555 bytes required raising size_limit above the 50000 default — user-approved; the 18-file sweep is homogeneous spec-prose work that belongs in one logical commit to preserve the cross-file consistency that motivated the parallel agent dispatch.

content.text:
Pausing for a scope decision before any edits — surfaces I'd silently expand into otherwise.

## Surface 4 findings — code state vs spec staleness

**Code state (verified):** Post-BBAAM Steps 1-5, the three RBRR fields are gone from `rbrr_regime.sh` enrollment. New shape:
- `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` + `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` are the only depot-identity inputs
- `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID = ${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- `RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY = ${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar`
- `RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM = ${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- `RBDC_GCS_BUCKET = ${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**Spec staleness in RBS0 (the "RBS0" half of the surface):**
- Mapping section lines 308, 310, 324: still register `rbrr_depot_project_id`, `rbrr_gar_repository`, `rbrr_gcb_pool_stem`
- Definition blocks at 2610-2617, 2629-2639, 2755-2776 describe these as live RBRR fields
- Line 1833: prose reference to `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` in vouch context

BBAAM Step 2 set the precedent: it dropped `RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` from the mapping section AND the definition block when the constant was dropped from code. The three RBRR quoins above were missed in that sweep.

**Spec staleness in RBSDE (the "RBSDE" half):**
The whole operation spec is stale, not just a banner. It describes:
- A CLI param `«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»` (now zero-arg)
- A timestamp generation step producing `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-«TIMESTAMP»` (timestamp dropped)
- A 30-char post-construction check (now lives in `zrbrr_enforce` as joint-length on CLOUD_PREFIX + 'd-' + MONIKER)
- Resource names `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-pool`, `-bucket`, `-repository` (now derived from CLOUD_PREFIX + MONIKER with different suffixes)
- An "RBRR configuration values" output banner exposing the three retired fields

**The expansion problem (cross-references):**
Beyond RBS0+RBSDE, the three stale RBRR quoins are referenced in 11 other vov_veiled files (24 references total): RBSGM, RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSSF, RBSSL, RBSAJ, RBSIW, RBSDY, RBSDI, RBSTB, RBSRR. If I delete the quoins from the RBS0 mapping section (matching the BBAAM precedent), those references render as literal `{rbrr_depot_project_id}` text — broken.

## Three options

**A. Strict reading of "RBS0 + RBSDE":** Drop the three quoin definitions from RBS0 + rewrite RBSDE. Accept that 24 references in 11 sibling specs will dangle until a separate sweep pace. Faithful to docket scope, leaves discoverable broken state in tree.

**B. Expand to "depot-identity sweep":** Drop the three RBS0 quoins + rewrite RBSDE + sweep the 11 sibling operation specs to rephrase references (use prose like "the depot project ID" instead of `{rbrr_depot_project_id}`). One-paddock-pass cleanliness; 13 files touched instead of 2.

**C. Replace with RBDC_* quoins:** Author new `rbdc_depot_project_id`, `rbdc_gar_repository`, `rbdc_gcb_pool_stem` quoins; sweep the 11 sibling specs to use the new names. Cleanest MCM model, but the docket explicitly says **"Mechanical prose update — not new quoin authoring"**, which I read as ruling C out.

## Recommendation

**B**, with the caveat that it's one notch larger than docket scope. The "RBS0 + RBSDE" framing reads to me as a mid-level estimate written before the cross-reference fan-out was visible. The reality on the ground is that the BBAAM Step 2 precedent (drop quoin → drop mapping → drop definition) was applied incompletely to RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID without realizing three sibling RBRR quoins also became stale. Sweeping the cross-references in B keeps the spec internally consistent and matches the spirit of "(CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) becomes the only described shape."

If you'd rather honor strict scope, A is fine — I'll note the dangling references for a follow-on pace.

Which do you prefer? Or is there a fourth option I'm missing?

file.content:
Step 1 (Alpine) writes the attestor public key to `/workspace/keys/`.
Step 2 (gcloud/Python 3) fetches provenance via `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance`, extracts the v1.0 DSSE envelope, decodes base64url payload + signature, reconstructs PAE, and verifies via `openssl dgst -sha256 -verify` subprocess against the embedded attestor key.
No slsa-verifier, no jq — Python `json` + `base64` modules + openssl subprocess only.
+
**Vouch Cloud Build job architecture**:
Vouch uses the RunBuild API (`builds.create`) rather than trigger dispatch — the hallmark is known only after the image arrives in GAR, so a pre-registered trigger cannot provide it.
The build runs as the {rbtr_mason} service account.
The vouch step branches on `_RBGV_VESSEL_MODE`: conjure runs DSSE provenance verification, bind runs digest-pin comparison, graft stamps GRAFTED.
+
Discovered constraints from implementation:
+
  * **Private worker pool**: The RunBuild API caller ({rbtr_director}) requires `cloudbuild.workerpools.use` to specify a private pool, a permission not included in `roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor`.
    {rbtgo_director_knight} grants `roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser` at project scope.
    Vouch runs on the same private pool as conjure (`RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM`), eliminating an untested default-pool-with-buildx permutation.
  * **gcloud exception**: Vouch verification (conjure mode) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe` for provenance retrieval (see {rbsk_no_gcloud} exception).
    All other steps use the GCE metadata server for OAuth tokens and direct registry REST API calls.
  * **Builder image selection**: Step 1 (Alpine) writes attestor keys (conjure) or is a no-op (bind/graft).
    Step 2 (gcloud/Python 3) runs mode-aware verification.
    Step 3 (Docker) assembles and pushes the vouch container (`docker buildx build --push` for multi-platform).
  * **GCB dollar escaping**: Step scripts embedded in Build JSON require `$$` for literal `$` and un-escaped `${_RBGV_*}` for GCB substitution variables, following the same `sed` pattern as conjure's `${_RBGY_*}`.
  * **DSSE verification**: Provenance envelope signatures verified against embedded GCB attestor public keys (`projects/verified-builder` KMS). PAE reconstruction + `openssl dgst -sha256 -verify` subprocess. No slsa-verifier, no jq — Python 3 standard library only.
    builds.create provenance achieves SLSA Build Level 3 (₢AvAAB research confirmed).
+
**Idempotent re-vouch**:
Re-running {rbtgo_ark_vouch} on a hallmark that already has a `vouch` package re-executes verification.

{rbrr_gcb_machine_type}::
Compute Engine machine type for the private worker pool, consumed at pool creation time by
{rbtgo_depot_levy}.
Default: `e2-standard-2`.
The pool resource holds this value; individual build requests do not specify machine type.

[[rbrr_gcb_pool_stem]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_string
{rbrr_gcb_pool_stem}::
Worker pool base name (suffixed with -tether/-airgap by derived constants).
Derived constants `RBDC_POOL_TETHER` and `RBDC_POOL_AIRGAP` expand this stem into full resource paths.
Must be set to the pool base name created by
{rbtgo_depot_levy}
for this depot.
Builds always use a private pool; the default shared pool is not supported.
`options.machineType` is omitted from the build JSON — machine type is a property of the pool resource.

NOTE: *Private pool is always required.*
Private pools are a prerequisite for VPC Service Controls and `NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS` hardening (see `RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc`).
Pricing is region-dependent: in us-central1/us-east4, private pool e2-standard-2 matches the default pool rate ($0.006/build-minute); in other regions the private pool rate is up to 1.4× higher.
This cost is accepted because the security prerequisite is non-negotiable and maintaining two code paths (conditional pool vs. default) doubles configuration surface.
+
Cloud Build supports two distinct machine type naming systems:
the default shared pool uses Cloud Build enum values (`E2_HIGHCPU_8`, `N1_HIGHCPU_32`) in `options.machineType` of the build JSON;
private pools use Compute Engine instance type names (`e2-standard-2`, `n2d-standard-4`, `c3-standard-8`) in `workerConfig.machineType` at pool creation.
Valid private pool families: `e2`, `n2d`, `c3` (availability varies by region).
Since Recipe Bottle uses only private pools, only the Compute Engine format is valid for
{rbrr_gcb_machine_type}.

[[rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_integer
{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}::
Minimum concurrent Cloud Build execution count.

[[rbrr_gcb_timeout]]

params.intent: Surface 4 of multi-fact-cult-verb-spec: RBS0 + RBSDE depot-identity banner sweep matching BBAAM Steps 1-5 code reality. (1) RBS0 mapping section drops three stale entries — rbrr_depot_project_id, rbrr_gar_repository, rbrr_gcb_pool_stem — matching BBAAM Step 2's precedent of deleting RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID from mapping when its bash constant was dropped from rbgc_Constants.sh. The three RBRR fields were similarly dropped from rbrr_regime.sh enrollment in BBAAM Step 1 but their RBS0 mapping/definitions were missed in that sweep. (2) RBS0 definition blocks deleted at the corresponding [[anchor]] sites: rbrr_depot_project_id (former lines 2610-2617), rbrr_gar_repository (former 2629-2640 — its body referenced rbrr_depot_project_id, gone with parent), rbrr_gcb_pool_stem (former 2731-2740). The standalone NOTE block on private-pool requirement that followed gcb_pool_stem (machine type naming systems, regional pricing, e2/n2d/c3 family validation) preserved in place since it ends with a binding constraint on rbrr_gcb_machine_type — load-bearing content for that surviving quoin. (3) RBS0 line 1833 prose reference to RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM rephrased to '(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)' derivation in the vouch-on-private-pool description. (4) RBSDE rewritten to match BBAAM Step 2 zero-arg, RBDC-driven shape: dropped axhopt_typed_parameter section taking «INPUT_DEPOT_NAME» (no CLI param now), dropped timestamp-generation step (timestamp eliminated by collapse), added 'Derive Depot Identity' axhos_step exposing the four RBDC values «DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»=${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}, «GAR_REPOSITORY»=${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar, «WORKER_POOL_STEM»=${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool, «BUILD_BUCKET»=${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}. Joint-length validation note added pointing at zrbrr_enforce as upstream guard (replaces the deleted post-construction 30-char check). Project-create step gains a NOTE on soft-delete graveyard collision being intentionally fatal under the (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) shape. Resource construction sites updated to reference the derived constants by name. Mason SA naming preserved as mason-«RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER» per paddock's Mason-SA-name-unchanged lock. Post-creation banner trimmed to single-line success + governor_mantle hint (matches BBAAM Step 2's banner trim). Tracking step description updated to 'per-moniker depot fact files' (matches BBAAM Step 4's rbgp_depot_list reshape). No new quoin authoring per docket constraint — used literal RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX / RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER and RBDC_* identifiers in prose rather than introducing rbrr_depot_moniker / rbdc_* attribute references in the mapping section. Out-of-surface staleness surfaced but not touched: RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc carries definition prose for the three retired RBRR fields (lines 31, 53, 58, 164); 11 sibling operation specs (RBSGM, RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSSF, RBSSL, RBSAJ, RBSIW, RBSDY, RBSDI, RBSTB, RBSRR) reference the deleted quoins via {rbrr_depot_project_id} etc. and will render as literal text post-edit; RBSDU-depot_unmake.adoc describes the same stale CLI-arg + INPUT_DEPOT_NAME shape (BBAAM Step 3 made unmake zero-arg too). These sit outside the docket's RBS0 + RBSDE surface and would need a separate sweep pace. Three remaining surfaces in BBAAQ (BUS0 multi-fact, RBS0 cult-verb, README glossary) pending.

content.text:
Notch landed (commit a67aefea). Here's the fresh-chat prompt:

---

```
mount ₢BBAAQ.

Context: Surface 4 (RBS0 + RBSDE depot-identity banner sweep) just landed in
commit a67aefea — verify via jjx_orient on heat BB and reading recent commits.
Three surfaces remain. Proceed with surface 1: BUS0 multi-fact convention
quoin family.

Surface 1 scope (from docket rev-2, basis f774fb7):
- BBAAO is the foundation pace whose code already landed (commit bffc58cb).
- Author MCM linked-term shape (attribute reference + anchor + definition
  with appropriate voicing) for the multi-element fact-file convention in
  BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc.
- Cover: type-as-extension naming, closed registry pattern, dual-write
  semantics, BUF_EXT_* constant convention (value matches name downcased),
  BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK-only registry; RB-domain extensions stay
  in rbgc_Constants.sh).
- Reference the BURH alias case (burh_list_capture in burh_regime.sh emits
  per-alias observation facts via BUF_EXT_ALIAS) as proof-of-shape.

Read actual code state in Tools/buk/buf_fact.sh and Tools/buk/burh_regime.sh
before authoring. Do not work from intent — the BBAAO commit body describes
landed reality. Apply CMK MCM linked-term shape per Tools/cmk/vov_veiled
guidance.

Discipline:
- Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices.
- Don't second-guess docket scope. "BUS0 multi-fact convention" means
  BUS0 only. Surfaced sibling-spec staleness gets noted as out-of-scope,
  not silently swept.
- Ask user before notching. Ask before moving to surface 2.
- Out of scope (do not touch): code (bash/Rust/yml), single-fact convention
  spec already in tree (revise only if multi-fact convention requires
  comparative documentation), handbook tracks rbho/rbhp (sibling pace BBAAR).

After mounting and reading the paddock + docket, propose your concrete
authoring approach (which quoin or quoin family, where in BUS0 it lands,
what voicings) and wait for nod before editing.
```

---

Paste that into a fresh chat. The next agent will jjx_open, mount BBAAQ, read paddock/docket, then propose its surface-1 approach for review.
